title: feat: migrate app deploys through myceli proxy
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Adds generic `*.pollinations.ai` to `*.myceli.ai` proxy forwarding for app subdomains.
- Reworks app deploys into origin and cutover phases so Myceli origins are verified before public domains move.
- Splits proxy production deploys into safe core-only and explicit full generated route deploys.
- Adds explicit KPI and economics ops routes outside `apps.json`.
- Documents the migration runbook and Cloudflare sequencing constraints.

## Validation
- `npx biome check --write ...`
- `npm run typecheck` in `pollinations-myceli-proxy`
- `npm test` in `pollinations-myceli-proxy` (8 tests)
- `node --check apps/_scripts/deploy-app.js`
- `bash -n apps/_scripts/deploy.sh`
- `node scripts/gen-routes.mjs` generated 14 app routes + 2 ops routes, 24 custom domains total
- `git diff --check`

## Notes
- No live Cloudflare deploys were run.
- `deploy:production` remains core-only; use `deploy:production:full` only after app and ops origins are verified.

gist.headline: Underground Networks
gist.blurb: We're upgrading our app deployment infrastructure to route through the Myceli proxy. Just like a real mycelial network, it's making our connections stronger and more resilient behind the scenes.
gist.summary: Migrated app deployments to route *.pollinations.ai subdomains through the *.myceli.ai proxy, splitting deployments into safe origin and cutover phases.
gist.impact: This internal infrastructure upgrade ensures safer, verified deployments for community apps and operational routes without affecting public uptime.
gist.visual_concept: Mycelium (fungal networks) acting as a proxy/network for the apps. Polly the bee interacting with glowing underground mushroom networks connecting to servers.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny miner's hard hat, inspecting a glowing underground network of lime green (#ecf874) mycelium and mushrooms connecting to small retro servers. Soft pastel gradient background with warm, earthy lighting. Chunky retro sprites, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
